case_title: PAUL D. LAPACHET ET AL VS. LARON SANDERS ET AL

docket_entries.description: MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-11-25 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER TO RESTRICT PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE RECORD. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. FIRST, THE MOTION IS PREMATURE BECAUSE DEFENDANT NEED NOT VACATE THE UNIT UNTIL DECEMBER 2025. PLAINTIFF WARNS OF THE CONSEQUENCES OF AN EJECTMENT JUDGMENT, YET THERE IS NO JUDGMENT IN THIS CASE. SECOND, PLAINTIFF DOES NOT EXPLAIN HOW IT IS NARROWLY TAILORED TO SEAL THE ENTIRE RECORD OF A CASE. FINALLY, THE COURT CANNOT MAKE THE REQUIRED FACTUAL FINDINGS UNDER CRC 2.550 BECAUSE THE ONLY DECLARATION IS FROM COUNSEL, WHO DOES NOT LAY THE FOUNDATION FOR HIS ASSERTIONS REGARDING TENANT SCREENINGS, BACKGROUND CHECKS, DEFENDANT'S ECONOMIC OPPORTUNITIES, AND MORE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-14-26 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

Justin Goodman, Esq. Plaintiff Paul D. Lapachet as co-Trustees of the Ruth B. Lapachet Living Trust U/A/D February 4, 2002

Hearing held.
Arguments heard.
The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER SEALING THE RECORD PER CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 2.550(D)

is DENIED. No evidence provided that any prospective landlord ever reviews case files involving prospective tenants that have not been reduced to a judgment; or that such case has any impact on a credit score. The Court notes that OSC Re: Dismissal is set for February 24, 2026.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Restrict Public Access To The Record Per California Rule Of Court 2.550(D)
Off Calendar Aug-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER TO RESTRICT PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE RECORD

Appearances: Morgan Sellers of Zacks & Freedman, PC (415.956.8100) for plaintiffs and moving parties; No other appearances.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. First, the motion is premature because Defendant need not vacate the unit until December 2025. Plaintiff warns of the consequences of an ejectment judgment, yet there is no judgment in this case. Second, Plaintiff does not explain how it is narrowly tailored to seal the entire record of a case. Finally, the Court cannot make the required factual findings under CRC 2.550 because the only declaration is from counsel, who does not lay the foundation for his assertions regarding tenant screenings, background checks, Defendant's economic opportunities, and more. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-03-2025 Continued To Sep-24-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
